name: Matza Balls
recipeIngredient: 3/4 tsp powdered instant broth (chicken or vegetable)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the matzah meal and salt.
2. In a separate small bowl, stir together the egg whites, club soda, parsley (if using), and onion powder with a fork until just combined.
3. Pour the egg mixture over the dry ingredients and mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until the dough is evenly moistened and holds together when pressed.
4. Refrigerate the matzah balls for at least one hour before cooking.
5. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
6. Form the chilled matzah ball mixture into 8 equal portions (about 1 1/2 inch diameter each).
7. Gently drop the matzah balls into the boiling water using tongs or a slotted spoon.
8. Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Do not remove the lid during cooking time.
9. Remove the cooked matzah balls from the pot with a slotted spoon and serve in soup.
Source: The low-cholesterol Passover cookbook put out by the "International" Havurah of Temple Emmanuel, Lexington, MA.
Notes:
I have not tried this recipe. In general, though, matzah balls keep well in the fridge, just warm them up before serving in soup.
